title: Hallmark Inspection Report Review – Rules Reference v2
sources: Hallmark Inspection Manual V2026.04.10 · QIMAone Instructions HMK HK V4 · QIMAone Inspection Type and Workflow Decision Matrix 2025-07-30 · Hallmark Felt Ornament Variation Guide 2020 · QIMA General Instructions – Tests & Specifications checks 2025

rules.field_location: Produced quantity / Packed quantity (PO & Product details section)
rules.what_to_check: The quantity recorded must match the Order PO QTY from the "Purchase Order Change" document, not the total order QTY. The IRF filled by the supplier is not a valid source.

scope.detail: cross-check against the PO document and the IRF

rules.error_example: PO states 15,000 EA; IRF states 15,200 EA; report records 15,200 EA → wrong, must use PO qty
rules.correct_example: PO states 15,000 EA; report records 15,000 EA

rules.field_location: Produced quantity / Packed quantity unit (PO & Product details section)
rules.what_to_check: The unit (CTN or PCS) must match the unit stated in the IRF. Produced quantity and packed quantity must use the same unit consistently.

scope.detail: cross-check produced qty unit, packed qty unit, and IRF unit

rules.error_example: IRF states unit = CTN; report records produced quantity in PCS
rules.correct_example: IRF states unit = CTN; report records produced quantity and packed quantity both in CTN

rules.field_location: Lot size used for sampling (Workmanship section)
rules.what_to_check: For PDQ products, the lot size entered for sampling must be the retail unit quantity (PDQ count × units per PDQ), not the PDQ count itself.

scope.detail: cross-check lot size against PDQ count and units per PDQ stated in spec

rules.error_example: 500 PDQs × 12 units each; report uses lot size = 500
rules.correct_example: 500 PDQs × 12 units each; report uses lot size = 6,000

rules.field_location: SAP Group Number field (Document Availability checklist)
rules.what_to_check: The SAP Group # in the report must match the SAP Group # on the purchase order.

scope.detail: cross-check report SAP Group # against PO document

rules.error_example: PO shows HMK9700; report records HMK9725
rules.correct_example: PO shows HMK9725; report records HMK9725

rules.what_to_check: When multiple specifications appear in one report, the inspection type and AOQL must be derived from the spec whose SAP Group # matches the order, not from any other spec present.

scope.detail: cross-check inspection type against the correct SAP Group # spec

rules.error_example: Report contains spec HMK9700 (AOQL 1.5%) and spec HMK9725 (AOQL 3.0%); PO references HMK9725 but report uses AOQL 1.5%
rules.correct_example: PO references HMK9725; report correctly uses AOQL 3.0%

rules.field_location: HMK Inspection Reason field (Document Availability checklist)
rules.what_to_check: The value selected must match the reason stated in the supplier's IRF. See valid values below.

scope.detail: cross-check against the IRF document

rules.error_example: IRF reason field is blank; report selects "CQS – Inspection audit selected lot"
rules.correct_example: IRF reason field is blank; report selects "Non-CQS or Non-SEP supplier"
rules.additional_markdown:
| IRF reason field | Expected report value |
|---|---|
| Blank | Non-CQS or Non-SEP supplier |
| CQS audit | CQS – Inspection audit selected lot |
| High attention | CQS/SEP – High attention item |
| New format | CQS/SEP – New format |
| Engineer request | CQS/SEP – Hallmark engineer request (email/specs) |
| Retailer request | CQS/SEP – Retailer/licensor request – Walmart / M&S / Costco / Australia / Walgreens |

rows.Expected report value: CQS/SEP – Retailer/licensor request – Walmart / M&S / Costco / Australia / Walgreens

rules.field_location: High Attention Product (HA) field / HCLP field (Document Availability checklist)
rules.what_to_check: If the order ships to USA Kansas City (KC), both HA and HCLP fields must be answered Yes or No per the product specification. If the order does NOT ship to KC, both fields must be N/A.

scope.detail: cross-check destination (PO & Product details) against HA/HCLP field values

rules.error_example: Destination = KC HKBO; HA field = N/A
rules.correct_example: Destination = KC HKBO; HA field = No (per spec); HCLP field = No (per spec)

rules.field_location: All photo fields throughout the report
rules.what_to_check: For HCLP products, the report must contain no product photos. Every image field must show a blank image with the remark "HCLP, no photo". This applies to all sections including defect photos.

scope.detail: scan every photo field in the report

rules.error_example: HCLP = Yes; report contains a photo of the retail product in the DAS check section
rules.correct_example: HCLP = Yes; all image fields show blank image captioned "HCLP, no photo"

rules.field_location: Inspector's remark / HA-specific checkpoint (Document Availability checklist)
rules.what_to_check: For HA products, the report must include a statement confirming the inspector communicated with the back office / Hallmark PE regarding special requirements.
rules.error_example: HA = Yes; no mention of PE communication anywhere in the report
rules.correct_example: HA = Yes; inspector remark states "Communicated with Hallmark PE [name] re: HA special requirements on [date]"

rules.what_to_check: For suppliers Lung Cheong (Indonesia), Starlite (Malaysia), PT IGP, and all Hallmark brand orders destined for the USA, destination must be "KC HKBO". For Costco, Walmart, Walgreens, QVC orders, the retailer name must be selected.

scope.detail: cross-check against PO and production site name

rules.error_example: Production site = PT IGP; destination field = "International"
rules.correct_example: Production site = PT IGP; destination field = "KC HKBO"

rules.what_to_check: For re-inspection reports, the reason for re-inspection must appear at the head of the report.
rules.error_example: Report is a re-inspection after a previous FAIL; no re-inspection reason stated at the top
rules.correct_example: Report header states "Re-inspection – previous report [ref] failed on workmanship [date]"

rules.field_location: SR Test Report Check (Document Availability checklist)
rules.what_to_check: SR checkpoint result must be "Passed". If not passed, the overall report result must be FAIL.

scope.detail: if SR = not passed, verify overall result is FAIL

rules.error_example: SR checkpoint = Failed; overall result = Pass
rules.correct_example: SR checkpoint = Failed; overall result = Fail

rules.field_location: SR Test Report Check — report date / validity (Document Availability checklist)
rules.what_to_check: SR report validity: standard products = 1 year; Toys & Children products = 4 weeks; Food-contact products (group HMK9700) = per PO. Check the SR report issue date against the inspection date.

scope.detail: cross-check SR issue date, product category, and SAP group

rules.error_example: Product = toy; SR report dated 6 months ago → expired (must be ≤4 weeks)
rules.correct_example: Product = standard gift; SR report dated 8 months ago → valid (within 1 year)

rules.field_location: SR Test Report Check (Document Availability checklist)
rules.what_to_check: If the SR report covers product format level (not SKU level), verify the format matches the inspected product and the report is within validity. This must be noted in the report remarks.
rules.error_example: SR report is format-level; no remark confirming format match in the report
rules.correct_example: SR report is format-level; remark states "SR report covers format XYZ, confirmed match with inspected SKU"

rules.field_location: SR Test Report Check (Document Availability checklist)
rules.what_to_check: If SR report result shows a failure but the factory provides a Hallmark override email, the checkpoint is acceptable. The override email must be uploaded in the report.
rules.error_example: SR fails; factory mentions an override email but it is not uploaded in the report
rules.correct_example: SR fails; override email from Hallmark uploaded as attachment in the report

rules.field_location: SR Test Report Check (Document Availability checklist)
rules.what_to_check: If the SRR shows 3 "No" for the 3 applicable tests, SR report is not required. The checkpoint must NOT be failed in this case; the report must document this.
rules.error_example: SRR shows 3 "No"; SR checkpoint is marked Failed because no SR report was provided
rules.correct_example: SRR shows 3 "No"; SR checkpoint is marked N/A or Pass with remark "SRR 3x No, SR not required"

rules.field_location: SR Test Report Check (Document Availability checklist)
rules.what_to_check: For re-run orders reusing an SR from a different PO, the report must confirm the supplier provided a material-change confirmation to the 3rd party lab. Exception: Toys, Children products, Food-contact = always need new SR per PO.

scope.detail: cross-check PO reference on SR report vs. current PO

rules.error_example: Re-run order; SR report references a different PO; no material-change confirmation mentioned
rules.correct_example: Re-run order; remark states "Supplier provided material-change confirmation to Intertek on [date]"

rules.field_location: SR Test Report Check (Document Availability checklist)
rules.what_to_check: If SR report is missing, the report must flag it and note that Andrew Chan (andrew.chan@hallmark.com) was notified.
rules.error_example: SR report missing; checkpoint simply marked Failed with no further remark
rules.correct_example: SR report missing; remark states "SR report not provided. Andrew Chan notified by email on [date]"

rules.field_location: FR Test Report Check (Document Availability checklist)
rules.what_to_check: FR checkpoint must show "Passed". If not passed, overall result must be FAIL.

scope.detail: if FR = not passed, verify overall result is FAIL

rules.error_example: FR checkpoint = Failed; overall result = Pass
rules.correct_example: FR checkpoint = Failed; overall result = Fail

rules.field_location: FR Test Report Check — report date / validity (Document Availability checklist)
rules.what_to_check: FR validity: Non-HA items = 1 year; HA items = 1 year but must be tested per PO. If FR is missing, the FR checkpoint must be failed.

scope.detail: cross-check FR issue date, HA status, and current PO reference

rules.error_example: HA = Yes; FR report is 8 months old and references a prior PO → invalid for HA, must be per PO
rules.correct_example: HA = Yes; FR report references the current PO and is dated within the same production lot

rules.field_location: Documents and Sample Availability checkpoint (Document Availability checklist)
rules.what_to_check: Ship test report must be on file and referenced. It is valid indefinitely unless there are changes in: supplier, production process, product or packaging material, packed quantity, or packing method. From 2024 onwards it is included as an appendix in the FR report. If missing, the Documents checkpoint must be failed.

scope.detail: cross-check ship test reference against FR report (2024+)

rules.error_example: Ship test not referenced; no mention in FR report appendix; Documents checkpoint = Pass
rules.correct_example: FR report appendix includes ship test; Documents checkpoint = Pass with remark "Ship test included in FR report appendix"

rules.what_to_check: The report must document a side-by-side comparison of production sample against the DAS. This section cannot be left blank or skipped. VAS is used for Keepsake (KPS) only. If a limit sample is in use, it must be referenced.
rules.error_example: VAS/DAS Check section left blank; result = N/A without justification
rules.correct_example: VAS/DAS Check completed; remark states "Production sample compared to DAS — color, construction, and labeling conform"

rules.field_location: VAS/DAS Check / Retail packaging sections (for HO orders)
rules.what_to_check: For HO orders, the report must confirm cross-check of: colorway SKU, colorway UPC vs. production package, package type and retail price, product image vs. spec, unit price consistency across DAS/specs/commit sheet/retail package, and copyright consistency.

scope.detail: applicable only when BU = HO (Hallmark Ornaments)

rules.error_example: BU = HO; no mention of commit sheet cross-check anywhere in the report
rules.correct_example: BU = HO; remark states "Commit sheet cross-checked: colorway SKU, UPC, retail price, copyright — all conform"

rules.field_location: Documents and Sample Availability / Workmanship section
rules.what_to_check: For HA and HCLP products, the PP report must be referenced. Defects noted in the PP report must be specifically addressed during the workmanship check. Packaging details must be compared against the PP report.

scope.detail: cross-check PP report reference against workmanship remarks

rules.error_example: HA = Yes; PP report not referenced; workmanship remarks make no mention of PP defects
rules.correct_example: PP report referenced; workmanship remark states "PP defects: [list] — confirmed resolved in current lot"

rules.what_to_check: When a carton contains mixed SKUs, the packing list must be used to verify the assortment and carton range. Any discrepancy must be clearly remarked.

scope.detail: applicable only when mixed-SKU cartons are present

rules.error_example: Carton contains 3 SKUs; no packing list referenced; no assortment verification remark
rules.correct_example: Packing list referenced; remark states "Mixed SKU carton verified against packing list — assortment and qty conform"

rules.subsection: 2.8 Visual Standard – PT. Camino factory (KPS only)
rules.what_to_check: For inspections at PT. Camino Industrial (Indonesia) on KPS items, the report must state that the PT. Camino Visual Standard was applied in addition to general Hallmark visual requirements.

scope.detail: applicable only when production site = PT. Camino Industrial

rules.error_example: Site = PT. Camino Industrial; BU = KPS; no mention of PT. Camino Visual Standard
rules.correct_example: Remark states "PT. Camino Visual Standard applied alongside Hallmark general visual requirements"

rules.subsection: 3.1 Correct inspection type per product / channel
rules.what_to_check: The inspection type must match the product category and destination channel per the decision matrix below.

scope.detail: cross-check product category (name/description), destination (PO), and inspection type in header

rules.error_example: Product = 3D Keepsake; destination = KC HKBO; inspection type = AOQL 3.0% Single Sampling → wrong, must be AOQL 1.5%
rules.correct_example: Product = 3D Keepsake; destination = KC HKBO; inspection type = AOQL 1.5% Single Sampling
rules.additional_markdown:
| Product category | Destination | Expected inspection type |
|---|---|---|
| 3D – HMK Ornaments, Gifts (excl. Plush) | KC, UK, HCA, AU, NL, Nihon | AOQL 3.0% Single Sampling |
| 3D – HMK Ornaments, Gifts (excl. Plush) | Dayspring, Mary & Martha | AOQL 3.0% Single Sampling (min. sample 85) |
| 3D – HMK Ornaments, Gifts (excl. Plush) | Walmart FOB | AQL 1.5 / 4.0 Level I |
| 3D – Gifts Plush | KC, UK, HCA, AU, NL, Dayspring, M&M | AOQL 1.5% & 4.0% Single Sampling |
| 3D – Keepsake (KPS) | KC, UK, HCA, AU, NL, Nihon | AOQL 1.5% Single Sampling |
| 3D – Keepsake (KPS) | Walmart FOB | AQL 1.5 / 4.0 Level I |
| 3D – Hengfeng Ceramic | All | AOQL Single Sampling +2 levels vs. standard table |
| 3D – Crayola | KC, UK, NL, AU, Nihon, Mexico, France | AQL 1.5 / 4.0 Level II |
| 3D – Fixtures & Dealer Service | KC, UK, HCA, AU, NL, Nihon | AOQL 2.5% Single Sampling; fixed sample = 15 |
| 2D – HA items | All | Single Sampling Plan |
| 2D – Non-HA, individually packed | Standard HMK | Double Sampling Plan |
| 2D – Non-HA, mixed-packed | Standard HMK | Single Sampling Plan |
| 2D – Dayspring | All | QIMA/HMK: Double Sampling; other: Single Sampling |
| 2D – Dayspring 3D items | All | Single Sampling (min. sample = 85) |
| PDQ products | All | Single Sampling; lot size = retail units |
> Note: "Keepsake Ornament" (KPSK) must use "Keepsake (KPS)" inspection type, not Ornaments/Gifts.

rows.Destination: KC, UK, HCA, AU, NL, Nihon
KC, UK, HCA, AU, NL, Dayspring, M&M
KC, UK, HCA, AU, NL, Nihon

rows.Expected inspection type: AOQL Single Sampling +2 levels vs. standard table

rows.Destination: KC, UK, NL, AU, Nihon, Mexico, France
KC, UK, HCA, AU, NL, Nihon

rows.Expected inspection type: QIMA/HMK: Double Sampling; other: Single Sampling
Single Sampling; lot size = retail units

rules.what_to_check: The sample size must match the correct sampling table for the chosen inspection type and lot size. Use the tables below. Lot size must not exceed 50,000 units per lot unless specs specify otherwise.

scope.detail: cross-check lot size, inspection type, and sample size field

rules.error_example: Lot = 8,000 pcs; AOQL 3.0% single sampling; sample size = 65 → wrong, must be 105
rules.correct_example: Lot = 8,000 pcs; AOQL 3.0% single sampling; sample size = 105; accept ≤ 5

rules.what_to_check: A 2nd sample is only valid if the 1st sample defect count fell between the accept and reject numbers (neither accepted nor rejected in round 1). If a 2nd sample appears but the 1st sample result was already accept or reject, it is invalid.

scope.detail: cross-check 1st sample defect count against accept/reject thresholds before accepting 2nd sample result

rules.error_example: Double sampling AOQL 3.0%; 1st sample: 0 defects (→ accepted); report shows a 2nd sample was taken anyway
rules.correct_example: Double sampling AOQL 3.0%; 1st sample: 2 defects (between 0 and 5); 2nd sample correctly triggered

rules.what_to_check: The acceptance number field must contain the threshold from the sampling table, NOT the actual count of defects found.
rules.error_example: Inspector found 0 defects; records C# = 0 (actual count) instead of the table threshold (e.g., 5)
rules.correct_example: Lot = 8,000; AOQL 3.0%; C# = 5 (from table), defects found = 0 recorded separately

rules.what_to_check: The sampling unit must always be the retail unit (Market Sale Unit), not the wholesale or shipper unit.
rules.error_example: Product packed 12 retails per wholesale; inspector sampled 30 wholesale packs instead of 30 retail units
rules.correct_example: Inspector sampled 30 individual retail units

rules.field_location: Critical defects count / Overall result (Workmanship section)
rules.what_to_check: Acceptance number for critical defects is always 0. If any critical defect is found, overall result must be FAIL and escalation to PE must be noted.

scope.detail: if critical defect count > 0, verify overall result = FAIL and PE escalation noted

rules.error_example: Critical defects = 1; overall result = Pass
rules.correct_example: Critical defects = 1; overall result = Fail; remark states "Escalated to PE [name] on [date]"
rules.additional_markdown: Critical defects (non-exhaustive): animal fur, human hair or body fluid, insects or insect debris, mold on packaging or product, sharp tools inside the product (cutter, needle, scissors).

rules.field_location: Sharp point / sharp edge checkpoint (Workmanship or checklist section)
rules.what_to_check: Sharp point/edge check must be recorded for ALL Hallmark products. All products are considered free of shape hazard by default. If a sharp point or edge is found, it must be reported and escalated to PE — it is not automatically classified as Critical, but PE must evaluate.
rules.error_example: Sharp edge found on product; report classifies it as Critical defect without PE evaluation note
rules.correct_example: Sharp edge found; remark states "Escalated to PE for evaluation — not pre-classified as critical"

rules.field_location: Functional defect count / Function Test result (Workmanship + Tests section)
rules.what_to_check: Functional defect count must be recorded separately. Accept if ≤ C#/2. If > C#/2, Function Test must be failed and count remarked. Escalate to PE.

scope.detail: cross-check functional defect count against C# and Function Test result

rules.error_example: C# = 5; functional defects = 4 (> C#/2 = 2.5); Function Test = Pass → wrong, must be Fail
rules.correct_example: C# = 5; functional defects = 1 (≤ 2.5); Function Test = Pass

rules.field_location: Minor defects field / Major defects field (Workmanship section)
rules.what_to_check: For AOQL inspections (all except plush), Minor defects must be reclassified as Major. There is no Minor defect tolerance for AOQL. Exception: Plush items use AOQL 1.5% Major / 4.0% Minor.

scope.detail: applicable when inspection type = AOQL and product is not plush

rules.error_example: AOQL 3.0% inspection; 2 Minor defects recorded in Minor field; not reclassified as Major
rules.correct_example: AOQL 3.0% inspection; Minor defects reclassified and counted in Major field

rules.what_to_check: Every Major defect must have 2 photos: 1 global view and 1 close-up.

scope.detail: cross-check number of Major defects against number of defect photo pairs

rules.error_example: 2 Major defects found; only 1 photo total in the report
rules.correct_example: 2 Major defects; 4 photos: 2 global views + 2 close-ups, each with a caption

rules.what_to_check: For defects longer than 1 cm, the defect description must include a measurement (length/area) and the photo must show a ruler.

scope.detail: check each Major defect description and its photo

rules.error_example: Scratch defect = 3 cm; no measurement in description; ruler not visible in photo
rules.correct_example: Description: "Scratch, 3.2 cm length"; photo shows defect with ruler alongside

rules.what_to_check: If no defect is found, the report must explicitly record "no defect found".
rules.error_example: 0 defects found; defect section left blank
rules.correct_example: 0 defects found; remark states "No defect found"

rules.what_to_check: For box cards or set products, only 1 defect is counted per box or set, regardless of how many individual items inside are affected.

scope.detail: applicable when product = box card or set

rules.error_example: Box of 6 cards; 3 cards have a minor print mark; inspector records 3 defects
rules.correct_example: Box of 6 cards; 3 cards affected; inspector records 1 defect (1 box = 1 unit)

rules.what_to_check: Minor print defects under 5 mm must not be reported unless they affect sellability. If they affect sellability, they must be classified as Major.
rules.error_example: 2 mm print smudge on inner page; recorded as Minor defect → should not be recorded at all
rules.correct_example: 2 mm print smudge on inner page; not recorded (below 5 mm, no impact on sellability)

rules.what_to_check: Dirt marks on kraft recycled paper material (without glue residue penetrating onto the bag surface) are acceptable and must not be recorded as defects.

scope.detail: applicable when product uses kraft recycled paper

rules.error_example: Kraft paper bag; surface dirt marks (no glue residue); recorded as Minor defects
rules.correct_example: Kraft paper bag; surface dirt marks noted but not recorded as defects per acceptance rule

rules.what_to_check: For felt ornaments and hand-crafted items, the following variations are acceptable and must NOT be recorded as defects: shape/width/thickness variation (if item remains recognizable), facial feature variation (if symmetrical and proportionate), design feature placement variation (if overall appearance is consistent), variation between units within a wholesale.

scope.detail: applicable when product type = felt ornament or hand-crafted item

rules.error_example: Felt ornament; slight asymmetry in embroidered eye; recorded as Major defect
rules.correct_example: Felt ornament; slight asymmetry noted; not recorded as defect — within acceptable variation for hand-crafted items

rules.what_to_check: For card items, boxed cards, and handle bags using the batch check method, at least 1 production sample must be fully compared against the DAS. Remaining samples are checked via batch method. The report must show this was done.
rules.error_example: Handle bag; batch check used; no mention of any individual sample fully compared against DAS
rules.correct_example: Remark states "1 unit fully compared against DAS; remaining 29 units checked by batch method"

rules.what_to_check: Measurement data must be recorded for at least 1 retail sample. No AQL applies. If a noticeable difference exists, a failure remark and a measurement photo are required.
rules.error_example: Measurement section left blank; no dimension data recorded
rules.correct_example: "Product dimension: L 14.8 cm × W 9.4 cm × H 4.7 cm (spec: L 15 cm × W 9.5 cm × H 4.8 cm) — within tolerance"

rules.what_to_check: For paper bags, handle length must be measured and remarked.

scope.detail: applicable when product = paper bag

rules.error_example: Paper bag inspected; handle length not mentioned in measurements
rules.correct_example: "Handle length: 28 cm (spec: 28 cm) — conforms"

rules.what_to_check: For products with components, component weight must be recorded in addition to product dimensions.

scope.detail: applicable when product has components

rules.error_example: Product has a detachable ribbon component; only overall product weight recorded
rules.correct_example: "Product weight: 85 g; ribbon component weight: 12 g"

rules.what_to_check: CMYK color result (PressSIGN report) must be ≥90%. If below 90%, the report must include a remark. If neither PressSIGN nor Delta E is available, the check relies on visual comparison and any doubt must be recorded as a failure.
rules.error_example: PressSIGN result = 84%; no remark in report; checkpoint = Pass
rules.correct_example: PressSIGN result = 84%; remark added; checkpoint = Fail with client notification

rules.what_to_check: For PMS / solid colors, Delta E (Delta E 2000) must be ≤2. If >2, the report must flag it and include photos of the color target Lab value and the Delta E reading.
rules.error_example: Delta E = 2.8; no flag in report; no photos of Lab value or Delta E reading
rules.correct_example: Delta E = 2.8; checkpoint flagged as Fail; photos of Lab value and Delta E reading included

rules.what_to_check: Packaging check must cover 18 units (shippers / wholesale / retail). If 1–2 defects are found, a 2nd sample of 100 units is required (118 total). The report must reflect which stage was reached.
rules.error_example: 1 packaging defect found at 18 units; no 2nd sample conducted; checkpoint = Pass
rules.correct_example: 1 defect at 18 units; 2nd sample of 100 conducted; combined result recorded

rules.what_to_check: Actual shipper dimensions and weight must be within the applicable limits. If exceeded, the shipper check must be failed.
rules.error_example: Standard HMK order; carton measured 60×55×90 cm → exceeds max; checkpoint = Pass
rules.correct_example: Standard HMK order; carton measured 50×45×70 cm → within limits; checkpoint = Pass
rules.additional_markdown:
| Channel | Max dimensions (O.D.) | Max weight |
|---|---|---|
| Standard HMK | L22"×W21"×D32" (min L12"×W8"×D5") | 50 lbs (min 5 lbs) |
| Walgreens FOB | L48"×W40"×H46" | 50 lbs (no minimum) |
| Dayspring | 24"H×24"W×14.5"L | 30 lbs |
| HCA (Australia) | Standard dimensions | 12 kg (15 kg for tag-on) |

rules.field_location: Shipper Dimensions & Weight & Marks checkpoint / shipmark photos
rules.what_to_check: Shipmark color must be BLACK. Both end panels must include: NET WEIGHT (kg), GROSS WEIGHT (kg), DIMENSIONS (cm), CARTON NUM, HALLMARK MARKETING COMPANY LLC, REUSE #, MADE IN. Font approx. 13 mm bold. Hallmark crown logo on side panel 1; shipping address on side panel 2.
rules.error_example: End panel missing REUSE # field; checkpoint = Pass
rules.correct_example: Both end panels show all required fields; shipmark is black; checkpoint = Pass

rules.field_location: REUSE # value on shipper end panel
rules.what_to_check: REUSE # = 4-digit inner volume in cubic inches. Must fall within the range [(L−0.5)×(W−0.5)×(D−0.5)] to [(L+0.5)×(W+0.5)×(D+0.5)] using inner dimensions in inches.
rules.error_example: Inner dims = 20"×16"×12" → range = 2907–3393; REUSE # = 2500 → out of range
rules.correct_example: Inner dims = 20"×16"×12" → REUSE # = 3072 → within range

rules.what_to_check: Corner label required for all finished-product shipments to Hallmark US and Canada warehouses. Not required for: sales samples, component orders, international orders (excluding Canada). Label must be on the right bottom corner of the crown-logo side panel, 1"–3" from the bottom, wrapping around two adjacent panels, within ±15° of parallel with carton bottom.
rules.error_example: Hallmark US order; corner label placed on top panel of carton
rules.correct_example: Corner label on right bottom corner of crown-logo side panel, 2" from bottom, correctly wrapping

rules.field_location: Carton Labels & UPC Check — label content
rules.what_to_check: Mandatory label content: SAP Material Number (top), Material Number/SKU (11-char, no international suffix), material description (exact match to PO/spec), Wholesale QTY (wholesale boxes in shipper, max 4 digits, NOT retail count), Partial quantity (blank or 0), Destination, Date code (MMYY, must not post-date shipping month), Vendor ID (GS preferred), Made In (2-letter ISO), Canada Price (if applicable).

scope.detail: cross-check each label field against PO and spec

rules.error_example: Wholesale QTY field shows retail count (e.g., 120) instead of wholesale box count (e.g., 10)
rules.correct_example: Wholesale QTY = 10 (10 wholesale boxes in shipper); retail count not shown on corner label

rules.what_to_check: SKU barcode scanning result must follow the 4-3-4 structure. Leading zeros to reach 4 digits in first and last parts; spaces after letter(s) to make middle part total 3 characters.
rules.error_example: SKU = 99TM2024; report shows scan result = "99TM2024" (no 4-3-4 formatting)
rules.correct_example: SKU = 99TM2024 → scan result = "0099TM 2024"

rules.field_location: Carton Labels & UPC Check — label corrections
rules.what_to_check: If corner label has incorrect non-barcode information (destination, date code, vendor ID, COO), an overlay label is acceptable. Barcode data errors require a full reprint; manual amendments to barcodes are not allowed.
rules.error_example: Barcode on corner label encodes wrong SKU; factory applies handwritten correction → not acceptable
rules.correct_example: Barcode encodes wrong SKU; label reprinted; new label applied

rules.field_location: Carton Labels & UPC Check / Retail Labeling section
rules.what_to_check: If an extra barcode (price info barcode) is present on the retail box, it must be scanned and recorded. Readable metadata → Pass; unreadable → Fail.

scope.detail: applicable only when extra barcode is present on retail box

rules.error_example: Extra price barcode visible on retail box; not scanned; not mentioned in report
rules.correct_example: Extra barcode scanned; remark states "Price barcode readable — Pass"

rules.what_to_check: Only 1 PFC per PO per shipment; must be the last carton number. PFC must have: 2 orange "PARTIALLY FILLED CARTON" labels on opposite corners, and a printed corner label showing the actual wholesale quantity (no manual amendments accepted). Actual packed quantity must match the corner label.

scope.detail: applicable only when a PFC exists

rules.error_example: PFC has only 1 orange label; corner label shows handwritten qty correction
rules.correct_example: PFC is last carton; 2 orange labels on opposite corners; printed corner label matches actual qty

rules.what_to_check: Required fields: USA SKU | Canada SKU | product description | retails per wholesale | production date (MMYY) | Made In | Vendor ID. Canada-only orders show only Canada SKU. Label must not be on the sealing area. For products going through Hallmark DC, updated wholesale labeling guidelines apply.
rules.error_example: Label missing production date; label placed over sealing area
rules.correct_example: All required fields present; label on side panel, away from seal

rules.what_to_check: Wholesale label not required for film/polybag wholesale only if ALL 4 conditions are met: (1) stock number on retail ≥3 mm tall, (2) stock number visible through film, (3) retail count countable through film, (4) retail UPC visible and scannable through film.

scope.detail: applicable when wholesale pack = film or polybag

rules.error_example: Film wholesale; no label; stock number on retail = 2 mm tall (below 3 mm) → label required but missing
rules.correct_example: Film wholesale; no label; all 4 conditions verified and noted in remark

rules.what_to_check: Each wholesale unit must be in a sealed LDPE ≥30% recycled bag (min. 40 micron / 1.5 mil) with visible stock number and scannable barcode. White wholesale label min. 3.25"×1.125" (83×29 mm), black on white, on end or side panel. 1FB inner carton: 420×170×205 mm ±0/−2 mm. Each shipper must contain only 1 stock number. Gross weight ≤12 kg (≤15 kg tag-on).

scope.detail: applicable when destination = HCA (Hallmark Australia)

rules.error_example: HCA order; wholesale bag is standard PE, not LDPE recycled; gross weight = 13 kg → both fail
rules.correct_example: LDPE ≥30% recycled bag confirmed; gross weight = 11.5 kg → Pass

rules.what_to_check: Retail packaging must match spec, DAS/VAS, and PO on: quantity, packing components, packing sequence. Materials must be clean, undamaged, free from smears and odors. Standard pass remark: "Conform to Specs, PO and approved ship test report."

scope.detail: cross-check retail packaging against spec, DAS, and PO

rules.error_example: PO specifies 12 retails per wholesale; actual pack = 10; checkpoint = Pass
rules.correct_example: Actual pack = 12 retails per wholesale; matches spec; checkpoint = Pass

rules.what_to_check: Minimum thickness >1 mil (0.0254 mm). If bag opening width (flat) ≥5 inches, suffocation warning mandatory in English + French + Spanish (not required for wholesale polybags). Warning repeats every 18 inches. "WARNING" in ALL CAPS, min. 4 mm height. Font size by bag perimeter: ≥60 in → 24 pt | 40–59 in → 18 pt | 25–39 in → 14 pt | <25 in → 10 pt.

scope.detail: applicable when product is in retail polybag shipping to US or Canada

rules.error_example: Retail polybag width = 6 inches; no suffocation warning on bag
rules.correct_example: Retail polybag width = 6 inches; suffocation warning present in EN/FR/ES; font meets size requirement

rules.field_location: Shipper Dimensions & Weight & Marks / photo section
rules.what_to_check: If a fragile label or marking is present on the shipper, the report must include a photo of it. Symbol standard: 75 mm tall, 31 mm from top flap score and 31 mm from corner score.

scope.detail: applicable when fragile label is present on shipper

rules.error_example: Fragile icon on shipper carton; no photo of it in the report
rules.correct_example: Fragile label photo included; caption references position and dimensions

rules.what_to_check: Packing method recorded in the report must conform to the ship test report, COP, and/or packaging specification.

scope.detail: cross-check packing method against ship test report reference

rules.error_example: Ship test specifies 3-layer carton; inspector records 2-layer carton; checkpoint = Pass
rules.correct_example: 3-layer carton confirmed; matches ship test; checkpoint = Pass

rules.what_to_check: As of April 2025, "Case Count: xx of xxx" must NOT appear on the Dayspring carton mark. Dayspring corner label: 4"×4", top right corner of long carton side, 1 per carton. Must include: DaySpring item number (1" font, scannable barcode), prime item number, carton quantity, merchandise description, date of manufacture. Item number = UPC code with first and last digits removed (10-digit result).

scope.detail: applicable when channel = Dayspring

rules.error_example: Dayspring carton mark includes "Case Count: 3 of 24" line → must be removed
rules.correct_example: Dayspring carton mark contains all required fields; no "Case Count" line

rules.field_location: Shipper Dimensions & Weight & Marks (international non-Canada orders)
rules.what_to_check: End panels must include: PO Number, Stock No., Description, Qty, Net weight (kg), Gross weight (kg), Dimensions (cm), Vendor ID, Production Date (MMYY), Made In, REUSE #. Font: 19 mm for PO# and SKU; 13 mm for other fields.

scope.detail: applicable when destination = international (non-Canada)

rules.error_example: End panel missing Vendor ID; Production Date in DD/MM/YYYY format instead of MMYY
rules.correct_example: All fields present; PO# in 19 mm font; other fields in 13 mm font; Production Date = 0526

rules.what_to_check: Report must document a side-by-side comparison of the production sample against the DAS. Result cannot be left blank.
rules.error_example: VAS/DAS Check section shows result = Pass but no comparison remark or photos
rules.correct_example: Remark states "Production sample compared to DAS: color, construction, labeling — all conform"; comparison photos included

rules.what_to_check: Spell check must be performed on logos, text, event numbers, and all characteristics on packaging, verified against DAS and spec. Checkpoint recorded as "Yes" (match) or flagged if discrepancy found.
rules.error_example: Spell Check = Yes; retail package contains "BIRTHAY" instead of "BIRTHDAY" — should have been caught
rules.correct_example: Spell Check performed; "BIRTHDAY" confirmed correct; checkpoint = Yes

rules.field_location: Retail Labeling, UPC & COO checkpoint / DAS comparison
rules.what_to_check: The Hallmark crown logo must be present and correctly applied to retail packaging where required by spec.

scope.detail: cross-check logo presence against spec requirement

rules.error_example: Spec requires Hallmark crown logo on front panel; logo missing on production sample; checkpoint = Pass
rules.correct_example: Crown logo present and correctly positioned per spec; noted in DAS comparison remark

rules.what_to_check: Copyright and licensing information on the retail product must match spec and DAS. Any discrepancy must be failed and remarked.

scope.detail: cross-check copyright text on product/package against spec and DAS

rules.error_example: Copyright year on retail package = 2023; spec and DAS show 2025 → discrepancy; checkpoint = Pass
rules.correct_example: Copyright year matches spec and DAS; checkpoint = Pass

rules.what_to_check: Applicable only to items with button cell batteries shipping to US/Canada. Both product and retail packaging must show compliant warning statements. Exception: heavily embossed/textured products — product marking not required. Not applicable to Keepsake 2024 products or toy products for children under 14 complying with applicable Toy Standard. Items without button cell batteries or non-US/Canada shipments → N/A.

scope.detail: cross-check battery type, destination, and warning statement presence

rules.error_example: Product has button cell battery; ships to US; retail packaging has no Reese's Law warning → must fail
rules.correct_example: Product has button cell battery; ships to US; warning on both product and packaging; checkpoint = Pass

rules.what_to_check: PID number on display boards must be checked and recorded for 2D products. If no display board → N/A.

scope.detail: applicable when product = 2D and display board is present

rules.error_example: 2D product with display board; PID Number checkpoint = N/A with no justification
rules.correct_example: PID number verified against spec; checkpoint = Pass; or "No display board — N/A"

rules.what_to_check: Must be recorded for ALL Hallmark products. All products are free of shape hazard by default. If a sharp point or edge is found (including areas not designed to be sharp), report and escalate to PE.
rules.error_example: Sharp edge found on decorative product (not intended to be sharp); recorded as Minor defect; no PE escalation
rules.correct_example: Sharp edge found; remark states "Escalated to PE [name] on [date] for evaluation"

rules.field_location: Additional Tests section / all test checkpoints
rules.what_to_check: All applicable tests must appear in the report with their result. Any test listed in the "Additional Tests" section of the booking or spec must be documented with supporting photos. If a test fails, number of failed pieces must be stated in remarks. If a test is not required, supporting evidence must be provided (reference to PP report, spec page, or email).

scope.detail: scan all test checkpoints; flag any applicable test missing from the report

rules.error_example: Spec requires a Shake Test; no Shake Test section or result anywhere in the report
rules.correct_example: Shake Test documented in Additional Tests section with result and 1 photo

rules.what_to_check: Abuse test samples (pull test, glue joint test) must be drawn from bulk separately and noted as such — not from the visual inspection sample pool.
rules.error_example: Pull test performed but no remark clarifying whether samples came from visual pool or separate bulk draw
rules.correct_example: Remark states "Pull test samples drawn separately from bulk, not from visual inspection sample"

rules.subsection: 8.2 Function check vs. formal function test
rules.field_location: Visual inspection section + Function Test (Tests section)
rules.what_to_check: A function check (1–2 cycles per sample) must appear in the visual inspection section. The formal function test (25 cycles per spec) must appear separately in the test section. These are two distinct checkpoints and cannot be merged. Sample size for FR KPS, Greeting Cards, Houseware formal function test = 1 unit.

scope.detail: verify both checkpoints are present and distinct

rules.error_example: Only 1 function result recorded for the entire report; no distinction between function check and formal function test
rules.correct_example: Visual section: "Function check — 1 cycle per sample — Pass"; Test section: "Function test — 25 cycles — 1 unit — Pass"

rules.field_location: Carton Labels & UPC Check / Retail Labeling checkpoint
rules.what_to_check: Every barcode on the carton and retail package must be scanned, with a photo in the report. Sample sizes: shipper carton = 1 carton (all barcodes on corner label); retail Greetings/Print Specialty/Gift/Ornament = 1 pc; retail International/Crayola/M&S = 10 pcs.

scope.detail: cross-check sample size, scan results, and grade against channel and product type

rules.error_example: M&S order; only 1 retail unit scanned instead of 10
rules.correct_example: M&S order; 10 retail units scanned; all grades ≥C; checkpoint = Pass
rules.additional_markdown:
Acceptance criteria:
| Metric | Pass | Acceptable | Fail |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1st scan rate | ≥90% | 75–89% | <75% |
| Decodability | A (>0.62) | C (≥0.25) | D (<0.25) |
| Magnification | 100% | >80% | <80% |
| Quiet zone | 1/4" | 1/8" | QZ/SS warn |
Minimum grade: C (standard); B for HCA. Consistency rule: same label alternating C/D over 10 scans — ≥7/10 pass = overall pass; <7/10 = fail.

rules.field_location: Do-it/Euro Hook/Hang Tab checkpoint (or Hanger Check)
rules.what_to_check: Applies to 2D products with a hook or Do-it, and 3D Keepsakes with a hook. Test: 3× product weight held for 1 minute. Result must be recorded as Pass or Fail; if failed, pull-off force data must appear in remarks.

scope.detail: applicable when product has a hook, Do-it, or hanger

rules.error_example: 2D product with Do-it hook; no hanger test result recorded; checkpoint = N/A
rules.correct_example: Do-it hook tested at 3× product weight for 1 min; result = Pass; recorded in report

rules.what_to_check: Result must be recorded. If failed, pull-off force data must appear in remarks. No photo required unless failed. If a DIY weight pull tester was used, the report must state this.
rules.error_example: Pull test failed; no pull-off force data in remarks; no photo
rules.correct_example: Pull test failed; remark states "Pull-off force: 1.2 N (spec: ≥3 N)"; close-up photo of failure point included

rules.what_to_check: Result must be recorded. No photo required unless failed. Not required for Gift Presentation & Package Expressions BU products, nor for fabric/plush items. For glass or ceramic décor not handled in the coated area: up to 25% coating removal is acceptable.

scope.detail: not applicable for Gift Presentation & Package Expressions or fabric/plush

rules.error_example: Ceramic décor; coating removal = 20% in non-handled area; checkpoint = Fail → should be Pass (≤25% acceptable)
rules.correct_example: Ceramic décor; coating removal = 20% in non-handled area; checkpoint = Pass with remark "20% removal, within 25% tolerance for non-handled area"

rules.field_location: Grammage of Paper and Paperboard checkpoint
rules.what_to_check: Result must be recorded for 5 samples. Acceptable tolerance: ±5% vs. label claim. A photo and the actual measurement data must appear in the report.
rules.error_example: Only 3 samples measured; no photo; measurements listed in remark but no data in structured field
rules.correct_example: 5 samples measured: 347, 352, 355, 349, 351 GSM; standard = 350 GSM; all within ±5%; photo included

rules.what_to_check: Result must be recorded per FR report instructions.
rules.error_example: FR report requires glue joint test; no result recorded in report; checkpoint absent
rules.correct_example: Glue joint test recorded in Additional Tests; result = Pass; per FR report instruction

rules.what_to_check: Results for 5 samples must be recorded: working current <200 mA, standby current <10 µA, voltage per supplier batteries, dB 75–85 dB (Keepsake). "Battery Powered Product Evaluation Report" from factory must be referenced. A photo of the recorded test values must be included.

scope.detail: applicable when product is electrical/has sound or light function

rules.error_example: Electrical ornament; FHSA test results not recorded; no photo of test values
rules.correct_example: 5 samples tested; working current avg 45 mA; standby 2 µA; dB avg 78 dB; photo of meter readings included

rules.what_to_check: Applicable to Hallmark UK, Hallmark UK Own Brand, Hallmark Australia, and M&S only. 10 envelope samples. Pass = fiber pull detected after 20 min. Fail = flap opens before 20 min, or no fiber pull after 20 min. 1 photo of tested sample showing tearing area required.

scope.detail: applicable when channel = HMK UK, HMK UK Own Brand, HCA, or M&S

rules.error_example: M&S order; envelope seal test conducted on only 5 samples; no photo of tearing
rules.correct_example: M&S order; 10 samples tested; fiber pull detected on all 10; photo of tearing area included; checkpoint = Pass

rules.what_to_check: Carton drop test is mandatory when ANY of the following apply: (1) Hallmark engineering requested it in spec or email, (2) product is ceramic or glass, (3) any product dimension >16", (4) item previously failed ship test even once, (5) any fragile note/icon/sign on packaging. If 1st lot passed, subsequent lots do not require it (unless a new trigger applies). Any breakage must be escalated to Hallmark responsible Engineer.

scope.detail: cross-check product type, spec/email requests, packaging, and prior ship test history

rules.error_example: Product is ceramic; no carton drop test in report; not flagged as missing
rules.correct_example: Product is ceramic; carton drop test documented in Additional Tests; result = Pass; no breakage

rules.what_to_check: All photos must be in landscape orientation and have a description caption. Date format in reports must be DD-Month-YYYY (e.g., 17-July-2020).

scope.detail: scan all photos for orientation, captions, and date format

rules.error_example: 3 photos in portrait orientation; 2 photos have no caption; date shown as "2026/05/11"
rules.correct_example: All photos landscape; all captioned; date = "11-May-2026"

rules.what_to_check: Minimum 3 photos required: (1) 45° view showing corner label position and shipping mark, (2) close-up of corner label (both sides in one photo if possible), (3) photo allowing easy count of wholesale quantity (rows/columns/layers visible).
rules.error_example: Only 1 carton photo; corner label not visible; no quantity count photo
rules.correct_example: 3 carton photos meeting all 3 requirements above

rules.what_to_check: Minimum 2 photos: (1) side view showing wholesale label/marking, (2) view showing number of retails inside one wholesale.
rules.error_example: 1 wholesale photo showing only the outside; retails inside not shown
rules.correct_example: 2 photos: wholesale label visible; open wholesale showing 12 retail units inside

rules.what_to_check: Minimum 3 photos: front view, back view, close-up of retail labeling. If already present in DAS check section, no duplication needed.
rules.error_example: Only front view present; no back view; no label close-up
rules.correct_example: Front, back, and label close-up photos present; or noted as already covered in DAS section

rules.what_to_check: 8–10 photos required, DAS and production sample in the same photo: front/back/bottom of retail package, front/back of product, additional side views if applicable, 1 group photo showing DAS alongside 4–6 production units.
rules.error_example: DAS photos show only the production sample; DAS not visible in any photo
rules.correct_example: Each photo shows DAS and production sample side by side; group photo with 5 production units + DAS included

rules.what_to_check: UPC scan on corner label = 4 photos. UPC scan on retail package = 1 photo. Each barcode must have a corresponding photo.
rules.error_example: Corner label has 2 barcodes; only 2 scan photos provided instead of 4
rules.correct_example: 4 scan photos for corner label barcodes + 1 for retail package barcode

rules.what_to_check: Every Major defect: 1 global view + 1 close-up. Defects >1 cm: ruler visible in photo; length/area in caption.

scope.detail: cross-check number of Major defects against defect photo count

rules.error_example: 1 Major defect; only 1 photo (close-up only); no global view
rules.correct_example: 1 Major defect; 2 photos: global view of product + close-up of defect area with ruler

rules.what_to_check: Verify the inspection type used matches the channel-specific requirement below.

scope.detail: cross-check destination/channel against inspection type and AQL values in report header

rules.error_example: Channel = M&S; report uses AOQL 3.0% → wrong, must use AQL Level II
rules.correct_example: Channel = M&S; report uses AQL Level II with Critical 0 / Major 1.5 / Minor 4.0
rules.additional_markdown:
| Channel | Standard | Critical | Major | Minor | Total |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Marks & Spencer | AQL Level II | 0 | 1.5 | 4.0 | 4.0 |
| Crayola | AQL Level II | 0 | Functional 0.4 / Visual 1.5 | 4.0 | — |
| Dayspring | AOQL 3.0% | — | — | — | — |
| Nihon JP orders | AOQL 1.5% | — | — | — | — |
| Nihon non-JP orders | AOQL 3.0% | — | — | — | — |
| 2D Walmart FOB | AQL Level I | 0 | 1.5 | 4.0 | — |
| Cracker Component | AQL Level II | 0 | 1.0 | 2.5 | — |
| Cracker (assembled) | AQL Level II | 0 | 1.5 | 4.0 | 4.0 |
| UK / HCA / Netherlands | AOQL 3.0% | — | — | — | — |

rules.what_to_check: Overall result must be PASS only if ALL conditions below are met. If any single condition fails, overall result must be FAIL.

scope.detail: cross-check overall result against all conditions above

rules.error_example: FR checkpoint = Failed; overall result = Pass → inconsistent
rules.correct_example: FR checkpoint = Failed; overall result = Fail
rules.additional_markdown:
**PASS requires ALL of:**
- SR test: Passed (or documented exemption)
- FR test: Passed (or documented exemption)
- Visual defect count ≤ C#
- Critical defects: 0
- Functional defect count ≤ C#/2
**FAIL triggered by ANY of:**
- SR not passed, invalid, or missing without exemption
- FR not passed, invalid, or missing without exemption
- Visual defect count > C#
- Any critical defect found
- Functional defect count > C#/2
- Barcode grade below C (or below B for HCA)
- Required test result missing from report

rules.what_to_check: PE escalation is required and must be noted in the report for: all FAIL results, functional defect count > C#/2, any finding of sharp point/edge/small part, carton drop test breakage.
rules.error_example: Overall result = Fail; no mention of PE escalation in any remark
rules.correct_example: Overall result = Fail; remark states "Escalated to PE [name] on [date]"
